hook.text: Dig a hole deep enough and you see layers: dark topsoil, pale subsoil, broken rock, and then solid bedrock below.

buildup.text: Soil horizons are distinct layers that form as materials move downward through water and biological activity.

discovery.text: The O horizon is organic litter. The A horizon is rich topsoil. The B horizon collects minerals leached from above.

twist.text: Not all soils have every horizon. Desert soils may lack an O horizon. Young soils may have only A and C layers.

climax.text: Soil horizons reveal a soil's age, climate history, and fertility. Reading a soil profile is like reading a biography.

punchline.text: Every soil tells its story in layers.

quiz.question: What is the A horizon in a soil profile?
